LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS' (THOMAS ROTH AND BLUM COLLINS LLP) PETITION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION IS GRANTED (SEE ORDER FOR FULL TEXT). PETITIONERS' REQUEST THAT WILLIAM CAHILL BE SELECTED AS THE ARBITRATOR IS DENIED. THE PARTIES SHOULD MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE SELECTION OF THE ARBITRATOR AND, IF THEY CANNOT AGREE, THEY MAY AVAIL THEMSELVES OF APPROPRIATE MOTION PRACTICE TO RESOLVE THE ISSUE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR # 6576 =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS THOMAS ROTH AND BLUM COLLINS LLP'S MOTION AND PETITION FOR COURT TO APPOINT ARBITRATOR OR FOLLOW PROCEDURES IN CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1281.6 IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 25, 2016 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302 FOR THE APPOINTMENT OF AN ARBITRATOR. THERE WILL BE A SINGLE ARBITRATOR TO RESOLVE ALL ARBITRABLE ISSUES RAISED BY THE PARTIES. THE ARBITRATOR HAS THE AUTHORITY TO DIVIDE OR BIFURCATE THE ISSUES BY PETITIONER OR IN ANY OTHER WAY OR NOT TO DO SO. PER CCP 1281.6, THE COURT NOMINATES THE FOLLOWING FIVE PERSONS: READ AMBLER, BILL BETTINELLI, ZERNE HANING, DAVID HUNTER AND RANDALL NEWSOME. BY EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WITH A COPY TO ALL PARTIES BY 4PM ON OCTOBER 24, 2016, COUNSEL FOR ONE OR BOTH SIDES MUST NOTIFY THE COURT WHETHER THE PARTIES HAVE SELECTED AN ARBITRATOR. IF THEY HAVE DONE SO, THIS MOTION WILL GO OFF CALENDAR. IF THEY NOT DONE SO, THERE WILL BE A HEARING ON OCTOBER 25, 2016. AT THE HEARING THE COURT WILL APPOINT THE ARBITRATOR FROM AMONG THE FIVE NOMINEES. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

The Parties reported that they met and conferred, that they narrowed it down to two Arbitrators and expressed their concerns. Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Petitioners Motion And Petition For Court To Appoint Arbitrator Or Follow Procedures In Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1281.6
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONERS THOMAS ROTH AND BLUM COLLINS LLP'S Motion And Petition For Court To Appoint Arbitrator Or Follow Procedures In Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1281.6 is CONTINUED to October 25, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 for the appointment of an arbitrator. There will be a single arbitrator to resolve all arbitrable issues raised by the parties. The arbitrator has the authority to divide or bifurcate the issues by petitioner or in any other way or not to do so. Per CCP 1281.6, the court nominates the following five persons: Read Ambler, Bill Bettinelli, Zerne Haning, David Hunter and Randall Newsome. By email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all parties by 4pm on October 24, 2016, counsel for one or both sides must notify the court whether the parties have selected an arbitrator. If they have done so, this motion will go off calendar. If they not done so, there will be a hearing on October 25, 2016. At the hearing the court will appoint the arbitrator from among the five nominees.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Petition Hearing For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TOM ROTH, TEL. NO. 1-415-293-7684 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONERS;
ATTY. RYAN BURNS OF THE ZFATY/BYRNS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-949-689-4775 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioners' (Thomas Roth and Blum Collins LLP) petition for an order compelling arbitration is granted. The claims asserted by petitioners are covered by the arbitration clauses in the parties' 2009 and 2010 representation agreements. There is no basis to conclude that the arbitration clauses are unconscionable or otherwise invalid or unenforceable. SNG's arguments that petitioners' claims lack substantive merit and the claims of Blum Collins are barred by res judicata are to be resolved by arbitration and are not a basis to deny this petition. Petitioners' request that William Cahill be selected as the arbitrator is denied. SNG has not agreed to Cahill serving as the arbitrator and petitioners cite no authority that SNG's silence on the issue binds it to the selection of Cahill. The parties should meet and confer regarding the selection of the arbitrator and, if they cannot agree, they may avail themselves of appropriate motion practice to resolve the issue. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR # 6576 =302/hk
